figures_detected.description: Illustration of broken tooth / cracked tooth root on timing belt
Illustration of cracked/worn back surface of timing belt
Illustration of worn side surface of timing belt, showing worn/round corners and frayed wicks
Illustration of worn teeth on timing belt, showing canvas wear and rubber layer wear, with rotating direction arrows

content_blocks.text: Visually check the condition of timing belt. Replace if any abnormality is found.

rows.item_to_check: Tooth is broken/tooth root is cracked.
rows.problem: See figure SEM394A — broken teeth and cracked tooth root illustrations
rows.cause: Camshaft jamming; Damaged camshaft/crankshaft oil seal

rows.problem: See figure SEM395A — cracked/worn back surface illustration
rows.cause: Tensioner jamming; Overheated engine; Interference with belt cover

rows.problem: See figure SEM396A — worn side surface illustration. Belt corners are worn and round. Wicks are frayed and coming out.
rows.cause: Improper installation of belt; Malfunctioning crankshaft pulley plate/timing belt plate

rows.problem: See figure SEM397A — worn teeth illustration with rotating direction indicated. Canvas on tooth face is worn down. Canvas on tooth is fluffy, rubber layer is worn down and faded white, or welt is worn down and invisible.
rows.cause: Poor belt cover sealing; Coolant leakage at water pump; Camshaft not functioning properly; Excessive belt tension

rows.item_to_check: Oil, coolant or water is stuck to belt.
rows.cause: Poor oil sealing of each oil seal; Coolant leakage at water pump; Poor belt cover sealing
